title: Payhip Alternatives for Restricted Countries: An Honest Side-by-Side Breakdown — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: If you're a digital creator in Nigeria, or any other country where PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, or Payhip are restricted, you're likely facing a major obstacle: how to sell your digital products without getting blocked or turned away. Every day, creators are forced to navigate a maze of online payment restrictions, which can be as frustrating as they are frustratingly familiar. It's like being trapped in a digital labyrinth with no clear exit.
What Each Option Actually Offers

PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip are all popular platforms for selling digital products, but they come with a catch: they exclude certain countries from using their services. PayPal, for example, restricts access to over 50 countries, including many in Africa and the Middle East. Meanwhile, Stripe has been known to freeze accounts and freeze payments without prior notice. Gumroad and Payhip also have their own lists of restricted countries, which can change at any time. In contrast, the Core Shop Engine offers a full backend with automated delivery, payment verification, and PDF receipts, all without the need for a payment processor account. This means that creators can sell their products without the risk of getting blocked or turned away.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip all claim to have 'alternative payment methods' for creators in restricted countries, but the reality is that these options are often clunky, unreliable, and difficult to use. Meanwhile, the Core Shop Engine takes a different approach: it's designed to work everywhere, without any restrictions or limitations. This means that creators can sell their products to anyone, anywhere in the world, without worrying about getting blocked or turned away.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip all charge fees for transactions, which can add up quickly. But the Core Shop Engine takes a different approach: it charges a one-time fee of $49.99 for access to its full backend, with no monthly fees, no transaction fees, and no hidden costs. This means that creators can keep more of their hard-earned money and sell their products without breaking the bank.
Creators in restricted countries can sell digital products without getting blocked or turned away with the Core Shop Engine.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip all require creators to manually handle transactions, which can be time-consuming and prone to errors. But the Core Shop Engine takes automation to the next level: it offers automated delivery, payment verification, and PDF receipts, all without the need for human intervention. This means that creators can sell their products quickly and efficiently, without worrying about the paperwork.
Which One to Choose Based on Your Situation

Choosing crypto-native infrastructure does not mean your buyers need to understand crypto. The Web3 Wallet Connect module generates QR codes for each supported coin, displays live USD-equivalent prices, and guides buyers through the payment flow in plain language. Most buyers complete a crypto payment in under two minutes, even if it is their first time. NOWPayments handles the transaction confirmation and fires the webhook that triggers automated delivery. From the buyer's perspective, they paid and received their product. From your perspective, you received funds in a wallet you control without any platform intermediary between you and your revenue. That is the practical outcome of building on the right infrastructure from the start.
